Title: D-amino acid aminotransferase1 regulates grain chalkiness in rice by modulating endoplasmic reticulum stress response
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OsDAAT1 promoter (genomic complementation); Ubiquitin promoter, constitutive (Ubipro:dsRNAi-OsDAAT1); Glutelin C promoter, endosperm-specific (GluCpro:dsRNAi-OsDAAT1); CaMV 35S promoter, constitutive (35Spro::OsDAAT1-GFP for localization)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in vegetative tissues, especially node; localized to vascular tissues of the first node; protein abundant in nodes and endosperm, lower in leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily expressed in vegetative tissues, especially node; localized to vascular tissues of the first node; protein abundant in nodes and endosperm, lower in leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: D-amino acid aminotransferase family; DAAT-like domain; PLP-dependent aminotransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible transamination among D-amino acids; converts D-Glu, D-Met, or D-Ser plus pyruvate to D-Ala, and converts D-Ala plus α-ketoglutarate to D-Glu in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: D-Glu; D-Met; D-Ser; D-Ala; pyruvate; α-ketoglutarate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream interconversion step in D-amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls homeostasis of D-Ala that indirectly affects grain quality via ER stress and storage reserve deposition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Excess D-Ala is associated with protein isomerization changes, ER stress, impaired starch/protein biosynthesis, and poor grain appearance quality
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grains at 9 DAF; exogenous D-Ala, D-Ser, or D-Met treatment; field grain-filling stage; hydroponic seedling treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; genomic complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; constitutive and endosperm-specific RNAi; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; LC/MS quantification of D-amino acids; subcellular localization; RNA-seq; proteomics; reciprocal crosses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for D-Ala homeostasis affecting chalkiness; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsDAAT1 elevates D-Ala in stems, nodes, and developing grains, induces ER stress, disrupts storage reserve accumulation, and causes maternally controlled grain chalkiness; elite promoter haplotype with higher expression is associated with lower chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation reduces 1,000-grain weight, plant height, tiller number, panicle length, branch number, seed setting rate, total starch, amylose, and protein; overexpression in Hap1 background did not further reduce chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chalkiness is maternally controlled; endosperm-specific silencing did not cause chalkiness, whereas constitutive silencing did. OsDAAT1Hap1 is an elite low-chalkiness promoter haplotype. Study infers OsDAAT1 mainly affects D-Ala rather than D-Ser metabolism in rice. wsd1 is an allelic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use elite promoter haplotype Hap1/Hap1a for low chalkiness breeding; prioritize promoter/haplotype selection or editing to optimize expression in maternal vascular tissues rather than simple overexpression in endosperm; useful for improving grain appearance quality
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA1426511; proteomics/stereoproteomics: PRIDE PXD059692
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDAAT1 (LOC_Os03g24460) was identified by map-based cloning as the causative gene for the daat1 chalky-grain mutant. 2. OsDAAT1 encodes a cytosolic PLP-dependent D-amino acid aminotransferase with broad in vitro substrate activity. 3. Loss of OsDAAT1 elevates D-Ala in maternal tissues and developing grains, alters peptide/protein isomerization, triggers severe ER stress, and disrupts starch/protein accumulation. 4. Grain chalkiness is controlled through maternal tissues, consistent with strong expression in node vascular tissue. 5. Natural promoter haplotypes modulate OsDAAT1 expression, and Hap1/Hap1a is associated with lower chalkiness and selection during domestication.
